result:
If I could change the world in one way, I’d make it so that every person could *directly feel the consequences of their actions on others* before acting—not as guilt or punishment, but as clear emotional understanding.

A lot of cruelty, neglect, exploitation, and indifference survives because distance makes other people feel abstract. If that distance collapsed—if harm felt real before it was done—I think politics, economics, relationships, war, climate choices, and everyday life would all change at the root.

Not perfect empathy. Not forced kindness. Just a world where no one could honestly pretend other people don’t matter.
